channel.title: INmaster
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INmaster, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Makers Are Building Back Against ICE
item.description: In hacker spaces and at their homes, creative protesters are laser-cutting and 3D-printing tools to resist an occupation.

item.title: Blackstone backs Neysa in up to $1.2B financing as India pushes to build domestic AI infrastructure
item.description: Neysa is targeting deployments of more than 20,000 GPUs over time as demand for local AI compute accelerates.

item.title: As AI data centers hit power limits, Peak XV backs Indian startup C2i to fix the bottleneck
item.description: C2i has raised $15 million as it tests a grid-to-GPU approach to reducing power losses in AI data centers.

item.title: African defensetech Terra Industries, founded by two Gen Zers, raises additional $22M in a month
item.description: Terra Industries, the African defense company, announced Monday that it had secured an additional $22 million in funding to further expand the busines...

item.title: These nonsmoking hotel guests got charged $500 smoking fees — here's how to avoid it
item.description: TPG’s ombudsman investigates a recent spate of myserious smoking fees caused by faulty smoke sensors in hotel rooms.

item.title: Crockpot Chicken Marsala
item.description: This crockpot chicken marsala brings restaurant-style flavor straight to your slow cooker. Tender chicken, mushrooms, and a rich, savory sauce…

item.title: What Happens When Blood Sugar Falls Too Low
Tips for Making Exercise a Daily Habit
7 Nutrition Red Flags to Know About
item.description: Feeling unusually tired or noticing subtle changes in hair, nails, mood, or appetite? These signs may indicate nutrition gaps worth discussing with yo...

item.title: The best credit card welcome bonuses of February 2026
item.description: Make sure these elevated and limited-time credit card offers are on your radar this month, or you could end up missing out.

item.title: Google’s AI Overviews Can Scam You. Here’s How to Stay Safe
item.description: Beyond mistakes or nonsense, deliberately bad information being injected into AI search summaries is leading people down potentially harmful paths.

item.title: OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger joins OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI said OpenClaw will live on as an open source project.

item.title: From 10% to 47%: Cardiac Survival Rates Surge in Texas City That’s Empowering Citizens to Save Lives
item.description: Over the past two years, the city of McKinney, Texas, has significantly improved cardiac arrest survival rates through a coordinated effort between it...

item.title: 8-Year-Old Maryland Girl Becomes Youngest Grammy Winner Ever with Daddy-Daughter Album
item.description: An 8-year-old Maryland girl and her dad have made history with an album that’s the musical equivalent of sunshine. Aura V. and her father, whose recor...

item.title: 9 Best Windows Laptops (2026): WIRED-Tested Laptops to Buy
item.description: Windows laptops have never been so good, and they're only going to get better as we move through 2026.

item.title: Magical Footage Shows Stag Strolling up to Lick a Man’s Camera Then Calmly Walking Away – WATCH
item.description: A new video from the Scottish Highlands shows a wild stag walking right up to a hiker, getting so close that his breath fogged up the stunned man’s ca...

item.title: The Best Samsung Phones of 2026, Tested and Reviewed
item.description: From flagship and budget to flipping and folding, Samsung’s Galaxy range spans the breadth of the smartphone cosmos. WIRED’s here to help you make you...

item.title: Two Lost ‘Jungle Book’ Original Paintings Found in Family’s Home After a Century–They had No Clue What They Were
item.description: Two original illustrations for Rudyard Kipling’s The Jungle Book that were lost for over a century have been discovered. The watercolors had been hang...

item.title: Good News in History, February 15
item.description: 80 years ago today, some simple workplace encouragement material was posted on the wall of several Westinghouse Electric factories in Pittsburgh, PA, ...

item.title: The Nothing That Has the Potential to Be Anything
item.description: You can never truly empty a box. Why? Zero-point energy.

item.title: Longtime NPR host David Greene sues Google over NotebookLM voice
item.description: The longtime host of NPR’s “Morning Edition,” is suing Google, alleging that the male podcast voice in the company’s NotebookLM tool is based on him.

item.title: Horseradish Pot Roast
item.description: This horseradish pot roast is pure comfort food with a flavorful twist. The beef cooks low and slow until fork-tender,…

item.title: 5 reasons to get Chase's Ink Business Preferred Credit Card
item.description: Plenty of benefits make Chase's Ink Business Preferred Credit Card worth considering, from a high welcome bonus to strong coverage for travel and car ...

item.title: Spirit Airlines launches new inflight trading cards
item.description: Spirit Airlines launched a new set of trading cards featuring its historic and current fleet and liveries. The new cards are only available on Spirit ...

item.title: This iconic ski-in, ski-out points hotel in Colorado just refreshed its rooms. We took a look inside
item.description: Come with us inside the ski-in, ski-out Park Hyatt Beaver Creek as we check out its renovated rooms.

item.title: Weekly Meal Plan #77
item.description: If the thought of figuring out dinner every night is weighing you down, this week’s meal plan is for you.…

item.title: One Pan Cheeseburger Rice

item.title: 3 Ingredient Cool Whip Candy

item.title: Vegan Gluten-Free Morning Glory Muffins
item.description: Say hello to morning glory muffins, made vegan + gluten-free ! A classic treat originally created by Pam McKinstry in 1978 at her Nantucket restaurant...

item.title: Why lunch is the main meal in some countries
item.description: For many travellers, dinner feels like the main event. It is where expectations concentrate: the moment reserved for something special, social, or mem...

item.title: 63 Essential Things to Know Before Visiting Tokyo
item.description: From trains to table manners, these are the lessons Tokyo quietly teaches travellers. They’re practical, cultural, and often invisible until you get t...

item.title: What is considered a “normal meal” in different cultures
item.description: People often search for a “normal meal” when they arrive somewhere new. Something familiar enough to feel grounding, but local enough to feel authenti...

item.title: What grocery stores reveal about how people eat
item.description: A grocery store is one of the most revealing places you can walk into when travelling. More than restaurants or cafés, it shows how people actually ea...

item.description: If you’re looking for a decadent, elevated hot chocolate with wholesome ingredients, this one’s for you! We’ve been serving up this frothy, tahini-inf...

item.title: Jobless claims fall to lowest level since mid-May
Jobless claims stay low in latest week
Consumer credit growth soars in December
U.S. productivity slows down in fourth quarter while unit labor costs accelerate
Beyond to buy rights to Buy Buy Baby brand and reunite it with Bed Bath & Beyond
